Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user has given a fairly typical process mining event log scenario They have a small event log with 5 cases tickets 101 105 each with timestamps of activities Receive Ticket Triage Ticket Assign to Level 1 Agent Investigate Issue Resolve Ticket Close Ticket Some cases also have an Escalate to Level 2 Agent event

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to analyze the event log to compute total resolution times duration from first to last event or from Receive to Close for each case Then compare

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 101 Received 2024 03 01 08 00 closed 2024 03 01 10 15 Thats a duration of about 2 hours 15 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 102 Received 2024 03 01 08 05 closed 2024 03 02 09 15 Thats roughly 25 hours 10 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 103 Received 2024 03 01 08 10 closed 2024 03 01 09 30 Thats 1 hour 20 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 104 Received 2024 03 01 08 20 closed 2024 03 02 08 30 Thats about 24 hours 10 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 105 Received 2024 03 01 08 25 closed 2024 03 03 09 30 Thats roughly 49 hours 5 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus case 105 has the longest resolution time case 102 has second longest case 104 third Cases 101 and 103 are short

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The average resolution time across the five cases is about 20 point 4 hours Cases 102 104 and 105 are above average However the question says significantly longer Likely 105 is the outlier while 102 and 104 are longer than typical but less extreme

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to examine the pattern of activities Cases 102 104 105 have much longer elapsed times than 101 and 103 Cases 102 and 105 include escalation while 104 does not

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 102 includes Escalate to Level 2 Agent after Assign to Level 1 Agent then Investigate Issue There is a gap between escalation at 11 30 and investigation at 14 00 and also a long gap between investigation and resolution from 14 00 on March 1 to 09 00 on March 2

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Possibly the investigation is delayed due to Level 2 being busy Also the ticket is escalated and there is a wait before Level 2 begins investigation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 104 has no escalation timeline Received at 08 20 Triage 09 00 Assign 09 30 Investigate 13 00 Resolve at 08 00 next day Close 08 30 So there is a large wait after assignment before investigating and another large wait after investigation before resolution

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This could be due to awaiting feedback internal approval or pending external resources

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 105 has escalation at 10 00 on March 1 then Investigate at 14 00 on March 2 then Resolve at 09 00 on March 3 So there is a very large gap between escalation and Level 2 investigation and another long gap between investigation and resolution

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Thus the pattern is that escalations cause significant delays because Level 2 can only investigate later Also after investigation the resolution step is delayed in several long cases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For case 101 durations are Receive to Triage 10 minutes Triage to Assign 10 minutes Assign to Investigate 40 minutes Investigate to Resolve 60 minutes Resolve to Close 15 minutes So all steps are quick

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For case 102 durations are Receive to Triage 25 minutes Triage to Assign 30 minutes Assign to Escalate 2 hours 30 minutes Escalate to Investigate 2 hours 30 minutes Investigate to Resolve 19 hours Resolve to Close 15 minutes Thus the longest wait is after investigation and there are additional waits around escalation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For case 103 the steps are quick Receive to Triage 5 minutes Triage to Assign 15 minutes Assign to Investigate 15 minutes Investigate to Resolve 30 minutes Resolve to Close 15 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For case 104 durations are Receive to Triage 40 minutes Triage to Assign 30 minutes Assign to Investigate 3 hours 30 minutes Investigate to Resolve 19 hours Resolve to Close 30 minutes Long waits appear before investigation and before resolution

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For case 105 durations are Receive to Triage 10 minutes Triage to Assign 25 minutes Assign to Investigate 10 minutes Investigate to Escalate 50 minutes Escalate to Investigate 28 hours Investigate to Resolve 19 hours Resolve to Close 30 minutes

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Main delays occur after escalation and between investigation and resolution not in closing the ticket The close step is consistently fast

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: From these few cases escalation appears to be a strong predictor of longer cycle time because both escalated cases are among the slowest and one of them is the clear outlier

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This suggests possible bottlenecks such as Level 2 capacity constraints Level 1 workload delaying investigation and dependencies after investigation such as approvals testing or external input

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Possible root causes are insufficient Level 2 staffing delayed handoffs during escalation poor prioritization after assignment and unresolved dependencies after investigation

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 104 is notable because it has no recorded escalation but still has long delays This may indicate hidden dependency waiting a bottleneck after investigation or a ticket that should have been escalated earlier

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Triage and close steps are relatively consistent and short compared with the large differences in investigation and resolution timing so the main bottlenecks are not at intake or closure

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Overall the repeated long gaps before investigation and especially before resolution indicate that waiting time rather than execution time is the main contributor to cycle time inflation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus case 105 is the most significant outlier and cases 102 and 104 are also longer than normal

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Escalation adds a handoff and a new queue If Level 2 is overloaded tickets wait longer before work resumes Long post investigation delays suggest waiting for approvals specialist input fixes or customer confirmation

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Recommendations include increasing Level 2 capacity or cross training Level 1 agents setting service targets for escalation response identifying ticket categories that should go directly to Level 2 improving queue monitoring and investigating why case 104 waited so long without escalation

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Additional recommendations are to monitor aging at each stage streamline post investigation approval or handoff steps and use dashboards or alerts when a ticket stays too long in assigned escalated or investigating status
